title: ocean-love.xyz — Product Roadmap

curiosity.psychology: Knowledge — I know this reef exists.

connection.psychology: Identity — This is my favorite reef.
connection.nature: Passive but personal — not action yet

conservation.psychology: Responsibility — I want to protect this reef.

design_rationale: See roadmap_chats.json — Connection features (favorites, logs, sightings) are not Conservation; merging loses the middle step where people become stewards.
north_star: The ecosystem behind every beach — not just a beach directory.
homepage_principle: Lead with discovery and beautiful ocean experiences. Weave conservation into every destination page so protecting the ocean feels like the natural next step, not a separate destination.

cycle_pattern.rule: Ship one full trilogy (3 epics) before starting the next trilogy. Each new trilogy begins again at Curiosity with deeper scope.
cycle_pattern.content_philosophy: Quality over quantity — pillar pages, validated data, lazy-loaded JSON; roll unfinished stories forward.

curiosity.goal: Traffic via search — people don't search for 'ocean conservation'

connection.goal: After discovery, user cares — saved places, stories, sightings, 'this is my reef'
connection.not_conservation: Threats and volunteer CTAs belong in Conservation epic — Connection builds belonging first

conservation.goal: Only after love — cleanup, donate, report, volunteer, impact tracking
conservation.bridge_copy: This reef has lost 40% of its coral. Here's how you can help.
conservation.note: Not homepage-first — woven into beach pages as 'Protect this place' after exploration

data_conventions.country_overview: data/explore/{country}/country.json — extend with marine overview + species refs
data_conventions.species_shards: data/education/species/data/{letter}-{n}.json — max 50 species per file, alphabetical series
data_conventions.link_rule: All cards (beach, country, habitat) reference species by slug; resolve via species index → shard file

cycles.name: Foundation — Discovery graph
cycles.progress: Epic 1 largely shipped for Aruba; Epic 2–3 not started.

epics.title: Country & beach discovery data
shipped: 21 Aruba beaches with live_rating, ecosystem slugs, search-index trie
Beach detail UI — spectrum bars, tide, rockiness, marine-life cards
Country marine_overview on Aruba + scaffolds for Bonaire, Australia
Sidebar country card — beach count, aggregates, species chips

stories.title: Country marine overview JSON
stories.ai_prompt: For each country in data/explore/, extend country.json with a marine_overview section: known_beach_count, aggregate ratings (swim, wave, snorkeling, scuba) on 0–10 scale, general ocean character, best_seasons[], and species[] array of slugs only (no embedded species objects). Align with existing explore/ lazy-load paths. Start with Aruba, Bonaire, Australia scaffolds.
deliverables: species slug refs linkable from country cards
UI country card shows aggregates + beach count
stories.completed: 2026-07-10 — Aruba aggregates computed from 21 beaches; Bonaire/Australia scaffolds; web/js/render-country.js + sidebar UI

stories.title: Sharded species catalog
stories.ai_prompt: Create data/education/species/ with index.json mapping slug → shard path. Species live in data/education/species/data/{letter}-{n}.json (e.g. b-1.json, b-2.json, b-3.json) — max 50 entries per file, grouped alphabetically by scientific or common name. Migrate existing marine-life animals/plants into first shards. Any beach card, country card, or habitat page links species by slug only; UI lazy-loads the shard file on demand.
deliverables: beach.json ecosystem arrays use slugs resolved through index
stories.remaining: 5 species cards exist under marine-life/ — migrate to sharded species/ layout

stories.title: Beach SEO landing pages
stories.ai_prompt: Expand beach cards into full discovery pages optimized for search: live_rating spectrum bars, rockiness, tide sample, tags, best_for months, visibility notes. Ensure static GitHub Pages deploy works. Target long-tail queries from user_journey.curiosity.example_searches pattern.
shipped: Beach detail template + 21 Aruba beaches in SPA

stories.title: Ecosystem & species context (interpretive, not CTA)
stories.ai_prompt: On beach pages, Connection section shows linked species cards, habitat story, seasonal highlights, and community photos/stories — build emotional attachment without volunteer/donate CTAs (those are Conservation). Like museum interpretation before the donation box.
deliverables: Species cards lazy-loaded from species shards
Connection UI block on beach page (web/connection context)

stories.title: Habitat profile pages
stories.ai_prompt: Habitat types: coral_reef, kelp_forest, mangrove, seagrass, open_ocean, tide_pool, estuary, salt_marsh. Each shows species, seasonal changes, best visiting season, community stories — conservation_status as context only, no volunteer links here.
deliverables: Beach pages link to primary habitat

stories.title: Saved places & visit log (local-first MVP)
stories.ai_prompt: localStorage MVP: favorite beaches, 'I've visited here', simple snorkel log (date, beach, species slugs seen). Foundation for future accounts. Lives under Connection — personal, passive.

stories.title: Protect this Beach + threats bridge
stories.ai_prompt: Conservation section on beach page: threats[] (plastic, ghost nets, bleaching), ocean_impact_score, upcoming cleanup refs, donate/volunteer/report links. Bridge copy: 'This reef has lost X% of its coral — here's how you can help.' Only shown below Connection content.
deliverables: Protect UI section on beach detail

stories.title: Ocean Cleanup Map MVP
stories.ai_prompt: Build cleanup map with layers: need level (low/moderate/high), event type icons (beach, dive, reef restoration, kayak, coral planting, ghost net, river). Each marker: org, volunteers needed, equipment, beginner friendly, cert required, next date, difficulty tier (family / snorkel / kayak / scuba / scientific). Static JSON index first; live updates later.
deliverables: Map UI at /web/cleanup.html or explore overlay

stories.title: Crowd reporting schema (future API)
stories.ai_prompt: Define report types: plastic, oil, ghost nets, bleaching, jellyfish bloom, dead fish, illegal fishing. Schema: GPS, photos, severity, verification status. Static samples in JSON for now; design for later submission API.

stories.title: Destination hub pages
stories.ai_prompt: Country hub pages for SEO: 'Best snorkeling in {country}', top beaches by score, season, species highlights.

stories.title: Live conditions integration
stories.ai_prompt: Live tide/wave API on beach pages; static fallback remains.

stories.title: Wildlife sightings & photo galleries
stories.ai_prompt: Users log species spotted at a beach with optional photo; displayed as community feed on Connection pages — personal celebration, not scientific submission (that's Conservation citizen science).

stories.title: Travel journals & stories
stories.ai_prompt: Short user stories tied to beaches — 'My first turtle at Arashi'. Community belonging features.

stories.title: Citizen science reporting
stories.ai_prompt: Structured submissions: species spotted, visibility, water temp, plastic found, coral health — verified ecological data, not casual photos. Links from Protect this Beach.

stories.title: Ocean Tech Directory & eco shop
stories.ai_prompt: Ocean-tech showcase (robots, interceptors, reef AI) + selective conservation marketplace (reef-safe sunscreen, sustainable brands). Mission-aligned only.

stories.title: Eco travel & cleanup org directory
stories.ai_prompt: Ethical dive centers, conservation liveaboards, cleanup orgs with map pins and skill levels.

stories.title: Live tide/wave data integration
stories.ai_prompt: Replace sample tide in beach.json with live API feed. Wave ratings update from buoy/forecast source. Keep static fallback.

stories.title: Global beach graph at scale
stories.ai_prompt: Expand countries beyond ABC islands; maintain lazy-load shard pattern for species and beaches.

stories.title: Newsletter & saved places
stories.ai_prompt: Users save beaches/species; weekly digest of conditions and cleanups near saved places.

stories.title: Impact reporting
stories.ai_prompt: Aggregate volunteer hours, reports verified, species observations contributed. Transparent impact on country/beach pages.

legacy_blocks_mapping.note: Former mile_stones block-1–3 align with Cycle 1 Epic 1–3 (curiosity beaches → connection species → conservation cleanup). Blocks 4–30 retired in favor of epic cycles; commerce phases remain in ocean-love.json distribution-strategy.
